user.title: Connect to Luxtronik 2.0
user.description: Enter the IP address of your heat pump controller.

data_description.host: Local IP address of the Luxtronik 2.0 controller (e.g. 192.168.x.x)

error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect to the heat pump. Check the IP address and make sure the controller is reachable on port 8889.

abort.already_configured: This heat pump is already configured.
abort.already_configured_benPru: A BenPru/luxtronik integration is already connected to this IP address. Running both integrations simultaneously will cause connection conflicts. Remove the luxtronik integration first.

init.title: Luxtronik 2.0 Settings
init.description: Adjust connection settings for your heat pump controller.

data_description.host: Local IP address or hostname of the Luxtronik 2.0 controller
data_description.poll_interval: How often to read data from the heat pump (10-300 seconds, default 30)

smart_energy.title: Smart Energy Features
smart_energy.description:
For heat pumps with multi-layer buffer tanks and DHW heat exchangers: Solar Boost uses surplus grid feed-in to heat the buffer tank. Night Heating Pause disables floor heating overnight to prevent the buffer from cooling down — ensuring hot water is available in the morning.

Leave 'Grid Sensor' empty to disable solar features.

data_description.grid_sensor: Entity ID of your grid power sensor (e.g. sensor.grid_total). Positive values = feed-in. Leave empty to disable.
data_description.solar_threshold: Minimum grid feed-in in watts before boost activates (default: 1500)
data_description.solar_normal_temp: Hot water setpoint during normal operation (default: 55.5)
data_description.solar_boost_temp: Hot water setpoint during solar boost (default: 65.0, limited by controller max)
data_description.solar_min_runtime: Minimum time boost stays active to prevent rapid cycling (default: 30 min)
data_description.night_pause_start: Time when floor heating is turned off (default: 18:00)
data_description.night_pause_end: Time when floor heating is restored to auto (default: 09:00)

dashboard_info.title: Dashboard Setup
dashboard_info.description:
A ready-to-use dashboard is available for this integration.

**How to set it up:**
1. Go to **Settings → Dashboards → Add Dashboard**
2. Choose a name (e.g. 'Waermepumpe') and icon (mdi:heat-pump)
3. Open the new dashboard → **⋮** → **Edit Dashboard** → **⋮** → **Raw configuration editor**
4. Copy the YAML from the repository file `docs/examples/dashboard-waermepumpe.yaml`
5. Replace `your-heatpump-ip` with your actual heat pump IP
6. Click **Save**, then **Done**

**Dashboard YAML:** https://github.com/notDIRK/luxtronik2-hass/blob/master/docs/examples/dashboard-waermepumpe.yaml

The dashboard shows operating status, temperatures, pump states, operating hours, setpoints, and controls.

error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect to the heat pump. Check the IP address and make sure the controller is reachable on port 8889.
